headline: Graduate Starting Salary Stagnant at RM2,300 for 8 Years
context: DOSM's Graduate Tracer Study 2025 revealed that median starting salary for fresh graduates remained at RM2,300 per month, virtually unchanged since 2017 in nominal terms and declining 14% in real terms after inflation adjustment.

cards.big: RM2,300 starting salary — same as 2017 but everything costs 22% more — fresh graduates are going backwards
cards.sub: A 2017 graduate and a 2025 graduate earn the same ringgit but the 2025 graduate buys 14% less with it

cards.big: Malaysia's graduate premium — earnings difference between degree and diploma holders — fell to 1.3x from 1.8x in 2010
cards.sub: Degree devaluation means 4 years of university investment yields diminishing returns compared to skilled trade pathways

cards.big: 42% of employed graduates work in positions not requiring a degree — underemployment worse in arts and social sciences
cards.sub: STEM graduates earn 38% more on average but still below Singapore's equivalent starting salary by 2.4x

cards.big: Higher Education Ministry produces no binding employment outcome data that universities must meet for programme accreditation
cards.sub: Universities assessed on research output and student intake — not on whether graduates actually find relevant employment

cards.big: When a degree costs RM80,000 but buys the same RM2,300 salary as 8 years ago, higher education is depreciating — not the currency
Should university programme funding be tied to graduate employment rates — with courses below 60% placement facing closure?
